metadata.guest_title: National Discipline Leader for Geotechnical Services
metadata.guest_bio: John Walton leads geotechnical services at Collier's Engineering and Design with 30 years of experience managing variable subsurface engineering projects. He specializes in team leadership and client communication.
metadata.definition_answer: Effective communication refers to the ability to clearly convey information, ideas, and expectations between individuals or groups to achieve understanding and collaboration. It involves both sending and receiving messages in a way that minimizes misunderstandings and builds trust within teams and with external stakeholders.
metadata.episode_intro: Effective communication strategies are vital for engineering leaders managing complex projects and diverse teams. This episode explores how clear, consistent communication fosters successful client relations and team cohesion. Listeners will gain insights into best practices for navigating project variability through education and trust-building.
metadata.episode_summary: This episode examines the role of effective communication strategies in engineering leadership. It highlights methods for managing client expectations, fostering team culture, and adapting communication styles to diverse situations in the AEC industry. The discussion offers practical advice for continuous improvement and building trust both internally and externally.
metadata.qa_items_0_question: What are effective communication strategies for engineering leaders?
metadata.qa_items_0_answer: Effective communication strategies for engineering leaders include maintaining consistent dialogue with clients and team members, educating stakeholders about project variables, and adapting communication methods to fit the situation. Leaders should also build trust by being transparent and approachable.
metadata.qa_items_1_question: How can engineering professionals improve client communication during variable projects?
metadata.qa_items_1_answer: Improving client communication during variable projects involves setting clear expectations about uncertainties, providing timely updates on unforeseen conditions, and involving clients in decision-making processes. Educating clients on technical risks and options helps manage project outcomes effectively.
metadata.qa_items_2_question: What role does team culture play in effective communication among engineers?
metadata.qa_items_2_answer: Team culture greatly influences effective communication by shaping how team members interact and trust each other. A positive culture encourages open dialogue, embraces diversity in communication styles, supports vulnerability, and fosters collaboration, which improves overall project success.
metadata.qa_items_3_question: How can engineering leaders develop their communication skills over time?
metadata.qa_items_3_answer: Engineering leaders can develop communication skills by continuously learning, seeking feedback, practicing humility, and adapting to the needs of different audiences. They should also observe effective leaders, practice active listening, and foster two-way communication channels.
metadata.qa_items_4_question: Why is two-way communication important in engineering projects?
metadata.qa_items_4_answer: Two-way communication is important because it not only allows the leader to inform stakeholders but also enables them to receive valuable input, clarify goals, and address concerns. This feedback loop ensures alignment and responsiveness throughout the project lifecycle.
metadata.qa_items_5_question: What are best practices for communicating negative or unexpected project news?
metadata.qa_items_5_answer: Best practices include informing stakeholders promptly, explaining the reasons clearly, presenting options for mitigation, and maintaining transparency. Building trust early helps ensure that negative news is received with understanding and cooperation.
metadata.qa_items_6_question: How can engineering leaders tailor their communication to different team members?
metadata.qa_items_6_answer: Leaders can tailor communication by recognizing individual preferences, personalities, and roles. Some team members may prefer detailed data, while others value big-picture insights. Adjusting tone, method, and timing helps improve engagement and effectiveness.
metadata.qa_items_7_question: What daily habits support successful leadership in engineering communication?
metadata.qa_items_7_answer: Daily habits include reviewing project status and financials, maintaining open-door policies, regularly checking in with team members, and proactively addressing challenges. Staying engaged and accessible fosters trust and effective communication.
metadata.qa_items_8_question: What advice would you give to new engineers about communication and career development?
metadata.qa_items_8_answer: New engineers should explore diverse experiences, remain open to learning, and develop communication skills early. Understanding the importance of clear dialogue with clients and teams will help them advance their careers and adapt to changing project demands.
metadata.mid_cta_headline: Enhance Communication Skills with EMI Training
metadata.mid_cta_body: Master effective communication in engineering leadership through EMI's targeted training programs. Develop competencies that improve client relations and team management for project success.
metadata.footer_cta_body: Join EMI training to sharpen your communication and leadership skills, paving the way for career growth and project excellence.

metadata.aeo_intro_html: Episode TCEP 309: Effective communication strategies are vital for engineering leaders managing complex projects and diverse teams. This episode explores how clear, consistent communication fosters successful client relations and team cohesion. Listeners will gain insights into best practices for navigating project variability through education and trust-building.
metadata.aeo_definition_html:
What is Effective Communication?

Effective communication refers to the ability to clearly convey information, ideas, and expectations between individuals or groups to achieve understanding and collaboration. It involves both sending and receiving messages in a way that minimizes misunderstandings and builds trust within teams and with external stakeholders.
metadata.aeo_qa_html:
What are effective communication strategies for engineering leaders?

Effective communication strategies for engineering leaders include maintaining consistent dialogue with clients and team members, educating stakeholders about project variables, and adapting communication methods to fit the situation. Leaders should also build trust by being transparent and approachable.
  • Communicate early and often with clients and teams.
  • Educate stakeholders about technical variability.
  • Use appropriate methods for urgency and content.
  • Build trust through openness and consistency.

How can engineering professionals improve client communication during variable projects?

Improving client communication during variable projects involves setting clear expectations about uncertainties, providing timely updates on unforeseen conditions, and involving clients in decision-making processes. Educating clients on technical risks and options helps manage project outcomes effectively.
  • Set realistic expectations upfront.
  • Keep clients informed throughout the project.
  • Explain technical risks and mitigation options.
  • Include clients in key decision-making.

What role does team culture play in effective communication among engineers?

Team culture greatly influences effective communication by shaping how team members interact and trust each other. A positive culture encourages open dialogue, embraces diversity in communication styles, supports vulnerability, and fosters collaboration, which improves overall project success.
  • Positive culture encourages open communication.
  • Trust allows vulnerability and sharing ideas.
  • Diverse styles require adaptive communication.
  • Strong culture improves motivation and cohesion.

How can engineering leaders develop their communication skills over time?

Engineering leaders can develop communication skills by continuously learning, seeking feedback, practicing humility, and adapting to the needs of different audiences. They should also observe effective leaders, practice active listening, and foster two-way communication channels.
  • Commit to lifelong learning and improvement.
  • Seek and act on feedback.
  • Practice active listening regularly.
  • Adapt communication to audience needs.

Why is two-way communication important in engineering projects?

Two-way communication is important because it not only allows the leader to inform stakeholders but also enables them to receive valuable input, clarify goals, and address concerns. This feedback loop ensures alignment and responsiveness throughout the project lifecycle.
  • Facilitates alignment on project goals.
  • Allows feedback and clarification.
  • Enhances responsiveness to changes.
  • Builds stronger relationships.

What are best practices for communicating negative or unexpected project news?

Best practices include informing stakeholders promptly, explaining the reasons clearly, presenting options for mitigation, and maintaining transparency. Building trust early helps ensure that negative news is received with understanding and cooperation.
  • Communicate issues early and clearly.
  • Explain causes and impacts honestly.
  • Offer solutions and options.
  • Build trust to ease difficult conversations.

How can engineering leaders tailor their communication to different team members?

Leaders can tailor communication by recognizing individual preferences, personalities, and roles. Some team members may prefer detailed data, while others value big-picture insights. Adjusting tone, method, and timing helps improve engagement and effectiveness.
  • Identify communication preferences.
  • Use varied formats and details accordingly.
  • Be mindful of tone and timing.
  • Practice empathy and adapt continuously.

What daily habits support successful leadership in engineering communication?

Daily habits include reviewing project status and financials, maintaining open-door policies, regularly checking in with team members, and proactively addressing challenges. Staying engaged and accessible fosters trust and effective communication.
  • Monitor project and financial indicators.
  • Keep an open-door for team discussions.
  • Engage regularly with staff.
  • Address issues proactively.

What advice would you give to new engineers about communication and career development?

New engineers should explore diverse experiences, remain open to learning, and develop communication skills early. Understanding the importance of clear dialogue with clients and teams will help them advance their careers and adapt to changing project demands.
  • Try different roles to find your passion.
  • Focus on improving communication skills.
  • Value clear dialogue with all stakeholders.
  • Be open to continuous learning.
metadata.aeo_mid_cta_html:
Enhance Communication Skills with EMI Training

Master effective communication in engineering leadership through EMI's targeted training programs. Develop competencies that improve client relations and team management for project success.

Learn About PM Training For AEC Professionals →
metadata.aeo_speakers_html:
Meet the Speakers

Your Host

Anthony Fasano, PE, AEC PM, F. ASCE

Most AEC organizations are too busy to focus on attracting, developing, and retaining talented professionals, making it hard to grow. Through his work at EMI, Fasano utilizes a proven framework to help organizations, both public and private, build professional development plans, programs, and tools that help them attract, build, AND maintain strong teams and grow sustainably. Fasano has written a bestselling book entitled Engineer Your Own Success (by IEEE-Wiley Press), and under his stewardship, EMI has built a massive content platform including 10 active podcasts, 3 YouTube channels, and a popular blog dedicated to helping AEC professionals become better managers and leaders.
Guest Expert

John C. Walton, M.S., P.E.

National Discipline Leader for Geotechnical Engineering at Colliers Engineering & Design
John Walton is the National Discipline Leader for Geotechnical Engineering at Colliers Engineering & Design. With extensive experience in geotechnical engineering and civil infrastructure projects, he has led investigations and design efforts for a wide range of developments, helping clients navigate complex subsurface conditions and manage geotechnical risk with practical, constructible solutions. Throughout his career, John has worked closely with multidisciplinary design teams, project managers, and contractors to deliver technically sound recommendations while maintaining clear and confident communication with stakeholders. His leadership focuses not only on technical excellence but also on building alignment across teams and fostering strong client relationships in environments where uncertainty is part of the process.
